However, qualitatively wi_skills produced higher-quality output despite the timeout: the sections are substantially longer and more detailed (e.g., 01 33 00 at 10,014 bytes vs. 6,338 bytes for wo_skills), include more Division 00 variety (Invitation to Bid, Supplementary Conditions amending AIA A201), and demonstrate deeper administrative specification craft such as detailed phasing milestones, AIA form references, and DBE payment prompt-pay clauses. The wo_skills TOC is also richer with a project summary block. Overall: wi_skills generates higher-quality individual sections with more professional clause-level depth, while wo_skills achieved broader coverage by completing without timeout. wi_skills is marginally superior in specification quality per section, but wo_skills delivered a more complete package within the run.

utility_details.utility_summary: Both wi_skills and wo_skills produced high-quality outputs that satisfy all 7 evaluation criteria. Both correctly applied targeted corrections (ASTM E283→E330 and VOC 100→50 g/L), preserved original clause structure, generated technically complete new envelope sections with proper MasterFormat three-part structure, embedded project-specific performance parameters (30 psf, SRI>=78, Climate Zone 5), cited multiple applicable ASTM/FM/CRRC standards, referenced specific drawing sheet numbers (A7.1, A7.2, R1.1, R1.2, R1.3), and included installer qualification and mock-up/testing QA requirements. Overall, wi_skills demonstrates marginally better edit-tracking discipline and avoids the ASTM E283 citation issue, making it slightly more technically precise, but the quality difference between the two runs is small since both met all evaluation criteria.

utility_details.judge_error: Both runs produced wholesale rewrites of the revised sections rather than making only targeted edits, which is a deviation from best practice for addendum specification revision. However, the specific corrections (ASTM E330, 50 g/L VOC) were incorporated. The file outputs were truncated in the provided logs, limiting full verification of the 09_91_13 VOC correction value; however, the addendum cover sheets in both runs explicitly state the 50 g/L correction was made.
utility_details.utility_summary: Both wi_skills and wo_skills produced functionally equivalent outputs that meet most evaluation criteria. Both correctly made targeted corrections (ASTM E330 substitution, VOC limit noted on cover sheet), both generated properly structured new envelope sections with MasterFormat PART 1/2/3 format, both included performance parameters (30 psf, SRI >= 78, Climate Zone 5), multiple ASTM standard citations, specific drawing sheet references, and installer qualification QA requirements. The wi_skills run used the csi-specification-writer skill invocation (Step 1), which appears to have guided a more disciplined approach — the air barrier section in wi_skills included a more complete mock-up/field QC section and slightly more organized REFERENCES structure. However, the practical difference in output quality is marginal. Both failed J3-2 (preservation of original content) as both appear to be complete rewrites rather than targeted edits. Overall, wi_skills is slightly more effective due to the structured skill guidance producing more complete QA provisions, but the difference is minor — both runs scored identically on 6 of 7 criteria.
